scope: Pass B full-surface expansion: every documented Close API endpoint (OpenAPI 3.1 spec at https://developer.close.com/openapi.json, 297 operations) is enumerated below, covered by a stream/write or explicitly excluded with a specific reason. Close's own OpenAPI spec publishes empty/generic request+response body schemas for most non-CRM-core resources (pipelines, statuses, groups, roles, custom fields, tasks, etc.) — schemas for those streams/writes were derived from documented query-parameter names, discriminated-union request bodies (e.g. CreateTask), and cross-referenced enum/sub-schema types (e.g. OpportunityStatusType) rather than a fully-typed response body.

excluded.reason: typed Call-activity listing; the base activities stream (GET /activity/) already returns every activity type including calls with a shared field envelope
logs an external call event; telephony-integration write, no demonstrated demand distinct from ordinary activity sync
single-call-activity detail already reachable via the call-activitys stream's listing; no separate stream needed for one extra fetch shape
updates a call activity's disposition/notes; no demonstrated write demand
deletes a call activity; no demonstrated write demand
typed Created-activity listing; a system-generated audit-trail subtype already visible in the base activities stream
single-created-activity detail already reachable via the created-activitys stream's listing; no separate stream needed for one extra fetch shape

typed Custom-Activity-instance listing; requires a specific custom_activity_type_id filter to be useful and the underlying custom activity TYPE definitions themselves (GET /custom_activity/) are out of scope below, so instances of an undefined type have no stable schema to declare
creates a custom-activity-type instance; deferred alongside the custom-activity-type read decision above
single-custom-activity detail already reachable via the custom-activitys stream's listing; no separate stream needed for one extra fetch shape
updates a custom-activity instance; deferred alongside the custom-activity-type decision
deletes a custom-activity instance; deferred alongside the custom-activity-type decision
